---
title: "Learner Profiles and Study Paths"
description: "Choose a course path based on your background, goals, and constraints."
execute:
  echo: true
---

## Why this matters

Different learners arrive with different goals, time, confidence, and access to
computers. A beginner-first course should offer safe paths without lowering the
standard for understanding.

::: {.questions}
### Questions

- Which path should I follow if I have never programmed before?
- What should I do if installation blocks my learning?
- How can I study if I have limited time each week?
:::

## Suggested paths

| Learner | Recommended path | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Absolute beginner | Start with Getting Started and Core Python in order. | Use Colab first to avoid setup friction. |
| Returning learner | Take the quizzes in Core Python and Data Structures. | Review any lesson where the quiz feels uncertain. |
| Data-focused learner | Complete Core Python, Data Structures, Functions, Debugging, then Data Science. | Do not skip debugging or data structures. |
| Project-focused learner | Complete through Projects and Environments, then start the Capstone. | Add tests and a README early. |
| Instructor | Use the lesson questions, challenges, and debugging corners as teaching beats. | Keep live coding small and participatory. |

## Beginner safety principles

- Use Colab when local installation blocks progress.
- Work in small examples until you can explain them.
- Save mistakes and fixes in a debugging journal.
- Ask for help with exact error text, code, and what you expected.
- Prefer steady practice over long memorization sessions.

## Reflection prompt

Write two sentences before starting:

1. "I want to learn Python because..."
2. "When I get stuck, my first debugging step will be..."


## Checkpoint quiz

::: {.quiz-box}
Use this OJS quiz to confirm the purpose of this support page.
:::

<script type="application/json" class="fcpython-ojs-quiz-config">
{
  "id": "learner-profiles",
  "title": "Learner Profiles and Study Paths checkpoint",
  "instructions": "Choose an answer, then check your work.",
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "main-idea",
      "prompt": "Which statement best describes Learner Profiles and Study Paths?",
      "options": [
        "Learners should choose a path that preserves the practice loop: predict, run, explain, modify, and debug.",
        "It is mainly about memorizing every Python command before writing code.",
        "It is a topic you can safely skip because later tools replace it.",
        "It is only useful after you already know advanced programming."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "The key idea is: Learners should choose a path that preserves the practice loop: predict, run, explain, modify, and debug."
    },
    {
      "id": "analogy",
      "prompt": "What is the purpose of the analogy in this lesson?",
      "options": [
        "To connect a new programming idea to an everyday experience.",
        "To replace practice with memorization.",
        "To prove that programming is only for experts.",
        "To avoid reading and running code examples."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "The analogy in this lesson is: A course support page is a guide rail that helps learners and teachers stay safe and oriented."
    },
    {
      "id": "debugging-habit",
      "prompt": "What is the best first debugging habit for this topic?",
      "options": [
        "Read the message or output carefully and change one thing at a time.",
        "Delete the whole program immediately.",
        "Change many lines before running again.",
        "Assume Python is random."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Small experiments make cause and effect visible."
    },
    {
      "id": "practice-habit",
      "prompt": "How should you practice after the first example works?",
      "options": [
        "Make one small variation, predict the result, run it, and explain what changed.",
        "Copy a large project without reading it.",
        "Skip practice and rely on recognition.",
        "Only read the final key points."
      ],
      "answer_index": 0,
      "explanation": "Prediction plus small variation builds durable understanding."
    }
  ]
}
</script>

{{< include _includes/ojs-quiz.qmd >}}
